mode_descriptions.bounded_internal_audit: Use synthesis as the starting compression, reread only the most decisive upstream artifacts, and avoid broad re-research by default.
mode_descriptions.targeted_escalation: Use a bounded internal audit plus a small number of targeted checks when a narrow ambiguity or meaningful edge justifies it.
mode_descriptions.not_ready_reopen_recommended: If responsible verification would require a much broader reread or new research pass, prefer a not-ready/non-action packet over pretending bounded verification was enough.

verification_policy.hard_rule: Decision-Maker is allowed to do targeted additional verification, but should not become a routine second synthesis pass. If adequate verification would require broader reread or new research, prefer a not-ready or non-action packet.

source_excerpt.decision_handoff_body:
# Decision summary

BTC finishing above $70,000 on the relevant Binance noon-ET one-minute candle remains the clear base case, but the swarm’s slight skepticism versus the 0.96 market is only weakly differentiated from market pricing because all meaningful evidence still collapses to the same Binance single-minute settlement mechanics. My post-synthesis view is that Yes is still very likely, but only marginally below the market rather than materially below it.

## Why this may matter now

Market-implied probability is 0.96. My syndicated range is 0.93 to 0.96. That is a high-Yes view with only a marginal or unclear edge versus market, not a strong contrarian one. The only plausible mispricing is that exact-minute settlement and single-source Binance dependence may deserve a small discount from near-certainty, but the discount is not strongly independently verified.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

The provisional swarm center was about 0.94. My final range is not materially different in center, but it is slightly compressed upward toward the market on the high end because the swarm’s bearish-to-market discount was only weakly independently verified beyond repeating the same exact-minute-risk argument across lanes. In other words, the swarm’s caution survives, but not as a strong anti-market edge.

## Edge verification status

Independent verification quality is medium, not high. The synthesis can treat as checked: Polymarket’s source-of-truth language, ET-to-UTC conversion, Binance kline open-time semantics, and the existence of a substantial pre-settlement cushion above 70000 during the research window. But the edge itself was not strongly independently verified because nearly every check traces back to Binance, the named settlement source, and there was no fresh near-settlement confirmation or independent evidence showing the market was specifically underpricing exact-minute tail risk.

## Compression toward market

Yes. I compressed toward market because the swarm’s slight bearish gap versus 0.96 mostly came from qualitative caution about exact-minute volatility rather than from a newly discovered hidden rule trap, fresh downside catalyst, or strong independent evidence that the market was too high. That caution is legitimate, but insufficiently verified to support a confident large discount.

## Timing and catalyst posture

The only catalyst that matters is the exact Binance BTC/USDT minute close at 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC. Any edge is likely to decay into that checkpoint unless BTC trades materially closer to 70000. Waiting for a near-resolution refresh would likely improve accuracy more than additional broad research.

## Key blockers

There is no major blocker to forming a view, but there are two caution flags: no near-settlement refresh on BTC’s live buffer, and minor residual uncertainty about whether Binance UI labeling could diverge from API/open-time interpretation. Neither is a major contract blocker, but both limit confidence in any claim of edge versus market.

## Best countercase

The best countercase, represented most clearly by variant-view and base-rate, is that 96% is too rich for a contract that settles on one exact Binance minute: a normal-looking overnight or pre-noon drawdown, or a small operational interpretation issue, could still create a No despite BTC being above 70k for most surrounding periods.

## What would change the view

A near-settlement Binance refresh showing BTC trading close to or below 70k would move the estimate lower quickly. Direct evidence that Binance UI candle labeling differs from the documented open-time interpretation would also materially lower confidence. Conversely, a final pre-noon check showing BTC still comfortably above the line with clean candle mapping would move the synthesis closer to or fully in line with market.

## Recommended next action

Request a final near-resolution check rather than rerunning the full swarm. If no fresh check is possible, hand off as a high-Yes, low-edge, freshness-sensitive market where caution is about timing precision rather than broad directional disagreement.

## Verification impact

Yes, synthesis used cross-lane verification and critically compared sidecars against raw findings. That did not uncover any major distortion in the sidecars; they appeared broadly faithful. The main impact was to reduce confidence in the more contrarian 0.91 lanes, because their discount versus market relied mostly on the same generic exact-minute-risk story rather than materially stronger fresh evidence. The failed external web search did not add anything.
